command_examples: ./scripts/subbrute.py example.com lists/names.txt | ./bin/massdns -r lists/resolvers.txt -t A -o S -w results.txt

documentation.readme: MassDNS A high-performance DNS stub resolver
usage_sections: Example: Resolve all AAAA records from domains within domains.txt using the resolvers within resolvers.txt in lists and store the results within results.txt: This is equivalent to: Example output By default, MassDNS will output response packets in text format which looks similar to the fo

arguments.name: -b
arguments.description: --bindto Bind to IP address and port. (Default: 0.0.0.0:0)

arguments.name: --busy-poll
arguments.description: Use busy-wait polling instead of epoll.

arguments.name: -c
arguments.description: --resolve-count Number of resolves for a name before giving up. (Default: 50)

arguments.name: --drop-group
arguments.description: Group to drop privileges to when running as root. (Default: nogroup)

arguments.name: --drop-user
arguments.description: User to drop privileges to when running as root. (Default: nobody)

arguments.name: --extended-input
arguments.description: Input names are followed by a space-separated list of resolvers.

arguments.name: --filter
arguments.description: Only output packets with the specified response code.

arguments.name: --flush
arguments.description: Flush the output file whenever a response was received.

arguments.name: -h
arguments.description: --help Show this help.

arguments.name: -i
arguments.description: --interval Interval in milliseconds to wait between multiple resolves of the same

arguments.name: --ignore
arguments.description: Do not output packets with the specified response code.

arguments.name: -l
arguments.description: --error-log Error log file path. (Default: /dev/stderr)

arguments.name: --norecurse
arguments.description: Use non-recursive queries. Useful for DNS cache snooping.

arguments.name: -o
arguments.description: --output Flags for output formatting.

arguments.name: --predictable
arguments.description: Use resolvers incrementally. Useful for resolver tests.

arguments.name: --processes
arguments.description: Number of processes to be used for resolving. (Default: 1)

arguments.name: -q
arguments.description: --quiet Quiet mode.

arguments.name: -r
arguments.description: --resolvers Text file containing DNS resolvers.

arguments.name: --rand-src-ipv6
arguments.description: Use a random IPv6 address from the specified subnet for each query.

arguments.name: --rcvbuf
arguments.description: Size of the receive buffer in bytes.

arguments.name: --retry
arguments.description: Unacceptable DNS response codes.

arguments.name: --root
arguments.description: Do not drop privileges when running as root. Not recommended.

arguments.name: -s
arguments.description: --hashmap-size Number of concurrent lookups. (Default: 10000)

arguments.name: --sndbuf
arguments.description: Size of the send buffer in bytes.

arguments.name: --socket-count
arguments.description: Socket count per process. (Default: 1)

arguments.name: --status-format
arguments.description: Format for real-time status updates, json or ansi (Default: ansi)

arguments.name: --sticky
arguments.description: Do not switch the resolver when retrying.

arguments.name: -t
arguments.description: --type Record type to be resolved. (Default: A)

arguments.name: --verify-ip
arguments.description: Verify IP addresses of incoming replies.

arguments.name: -w
arguments.description: --outfile Write to the specified output file instead of standard output.

arguments.name: --target
arguments.description: Target host, IP, MAC, or URL the tool operates on.

arguments.name: --help
arguments.description: Show help / usage and exit.

arguments.name: --output
arguments.description: Output file path for the report or capture.

arguments.name: --version
arguments.description: Print the tool version and exit.

arguments.name: --port
arguments.description: Target port (default: service default).

arguments.name: --interface
arguments.description: Network interface to bind to.

arguments.name: --verbose
arguments.description: Increase log verbosity (repeatable).

arguments.name: --threads
arguments.description: Number of concurrent workers.

arguments.name: --timeout
arguments.description: Per-operation timeout in seconds.

arguments.name: --rate
arguments.description: Packet / request rate limit.

arguments.name: --wordlist
arguments.description: Path to a wordlist (users, passwords, paths).

arguments.name: --proxy
arguments.description: HTTP/SOCKS proxy URL.

arguments.name: --user-agent
arguments.description: HTTP User-Agent header.

arguments.name: --cookie
arguments.description: HTTP Cookie header.

arguments.name: --header
arguments.description: Extra HTTP header (key:value).

arguments.name: --insecure
arguments.description: Skip TLS verification (lab use only).

arguments.name: --json
arguments.description: Emit JSON output (machine-readable).

arguments.name: --no-color
arguments.description: Disable ANSI color in output.

arguments.name: --quiet
arguments.description: Suppress non-error output.

arguments.name: --dry-run
arguments.description: Print what would be done without making any changes.

arguments.name: --yes
arguments.description: Skip confirmation prompts (use with care).

arguments.name: --force
arguments.description: Force the action even if a safety check would block it.

arguments.name: --no-banner
arguments.description: Suppress banner / tool-name output.

documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'any' phase against surfaces [web] in the recon category. Fits when the engagement needs: MassDNS A high-performance DNS stub resolver.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: MassDNS A high-performance DNS stub resolver.
documentation.how_to_use: Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `Usage: ./bin/massdns [options] [domainlist]`. Further invocations from upstream: `domain. (Default: 500)`; `./bin/massdns -r lists/resolvers.txt -t AAAA domains.txt > results.txt`. MassDNS A high-performance DNS stub resolver.

name: massdns
summary: MassDNS A high-performance DNS stub resolver

trust.warning: Attribution / index entry only. The operator must audit the code, the README, the license, and the releases before invoking. Phase 5 — entry is auto-generated from the operator's curated fetch list; no automated enrichment yet.

use_cases: When you need recon, use massdns because MassDNS A high-performance DNS stub resolver.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `Usage: ./bin/massdns [options] [domainlist]` as documented upstream for massdns.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `domain. (Default: 500)` as documented upstream for massdns.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `./bin/massdns -r lists/resolvers.txt -t AAAA domains.txt > results.txt` as documented upstream for massdns.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `./bin/massdns -r lists/resolvers.txt -t AAAA -w results.txt domains.txt` as documented upstream for massdns.
When chaining recon tools, pair massdns with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `massdns --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
